Hospital.title: Hospital
Hospital.description: Represents a hospital entity with its core information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the hospital entity.

history.description: A description of the hospital's history.

awards.description: A description of the hospital's awards.

socialContributions.description: A description of the hospital's social contributions.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a medical service offered by the hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Service)

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., LASIK, retina surgery).

description.description: A detailed description of the service.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a doctor or medical professional associated with the hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the doctor entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Doctor)

bio.description: A short biography of the doctor.

Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient registered at the hospital.

fullName.description: The full name of the patient.

emergencyContactName.description: Name of the emergency contact person.

emergencyContactNumber.description: Phone number of the emergency contact person.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the registration was submitted.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment scheduled by a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the appointment entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient. (Relationship: Patient 1:N Appointment)

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Appointment)

doctorId.description: Reference to the Doctor. (Relationship: Doctor 1:N Appointment)

serviceId.description: Reference to the Service. (Relationship: Service 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: The date and time of the appointment.

notes.description: Any notes related to the appointment.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Represents a patient testimonial for the hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the testimonial entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Testimonial)

patientName.description: The name of the patient providing the testimonial.

content.description: The text content of the testimonial.

date.description: The date the testimonial was given.

FAQ.title: FAQ
FAQ.description: Represents a frequently asked question and its answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FAQ entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N FAQ)

NewsArticle.title: NewsArticle
NewsArticle.description: Represents a news article or announcement related to the hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the news article entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N NewsArticle)

title.description: The title of the news article.

content.description: The text content of the news article.

publishDate.description: The date the news article was published.

Donation.title: Donation
Donation.description: Represents a donation made to the hospital.

id.description: Unique identifier for the donation entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Donation)

donationDate.description: The date the donation was made.

Volunteer.title: Volunteer
Volunteer.description: Represents a volunteer application or record.

id.description: Unique identifier for the volunteer entity.

hospitalId.description: Reference to the Hospital. (Relationship: Hospital 1:N Volunteer)

email.description: The email address of the volunteer.

phone.description: The phone number of the volunteer.

definition.description: Stores patient registration information. This is a top-level collection.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient registration.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support a patient-friendly hospital website. It is structured to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability while adhering to the defined principles. The primary strategy is denormalization to avoid `get()` calls in security rules and enforce authorization independence.

*   **Hospitals Collection:** The root collection for hospitals. All other collections are subcollections of a specific hospital, enabling data isolation and multi-tenancy.
*   **Services, Doctors, Patients, Appointments, Testimonials, FAQs, NewsArticles, Donations, Volunteers:** These are subcollections of the `/hospitals/{hospitalId}` collection. This structure facilitates easy querying of entities related to a specific hospital and maintains a clear hierarchy.

**Authorization Independence and QAPs:**

*   Authorization Independence is achieved by storing roles in the database. There are no custom claims.
*   The application uses path-based ownership for `hospitals/{hospitalId}` for private data. All the other collections, are subcollections under a hospital, making security rules for these subcollections straightforward.
*   This design supports QAPs by segregating data based on hospital. Listing data for a specific hospital is efficient because all relevant data is contained within the hospital's document path.
